All goals must be occupied simultaneously to trigger a WIN and advance to the next level. The game ends when all levels are completed.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to move a selectable piece (the salmon-colored block) onto each goal tile (marked as '+' in the semantic grid) in every level. All goals must be occupied simultaneously to trigger a WIN and advance to the next level. The game ends when all levels are completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: My piece moves on a squared grid. I can either click an empty, reachable tile to pathfind there (if unobstructed), or use MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT to shift one step along a horizontal path. The piece cannot pass through walls or obstacles (dark brown tiles). I can only select and move one active piece per level; other pieces are static and act as obstacles. Only when my piece ends its movement directly on top of a goal tile does it count as placed; if multiple goals exist, I must occupy all of them at once to win.
